title: How to Start Strength Training: A Complete Beginner's Guide
excerpt: Everything you need to know about starting your strength training journey, from choosing the right programme to mastering the big four lifts.
content: Starting strength training can feel overwhelming. Walk into any gym and you'll see dozens of machines, free weights, and people who seem to know exactly what they're doing. But here's the truth: every expert was once a beginner, and the fundamentals of strength training are actually quite simple...
author: James Mitchell
authorBio: James Mitchell is a Senior Strength Coach at VitalEdge with 12 years of experience in strength development and powerlifting.

title: 10 Nutrition Myths That Are Holding You Back
excerpt: From 'carbs make you fat' to 'you need to eat every 2 hours' — a registered dietitian separates fact from fiction.
content: The nutrition industry is built on confusion. Every week, a new diet trend promises to be the answer, and every influencer has a different opinion on what you should be eating. As a registered dietitian, I've spent the last 9 years cutting through the noise with science...
author: David Okonkwo
authorBio: David Okonkwo is a Registered Dietitian specialising in sports nutrition and body composition at VitalEdge.

title: Why Recovery Matters More Than Your Workout
excerpt: The science behind sleep, rest days, and active recovery — and why neglecting them is sabotaging your progress.
content: You don't get stronger during your workout. You get stronger during the hours and days after it. Every training session creates microscopic damage to your muscle fibres. It's during recovery that your body repairs this damage, building back stronger than before...
author: Sarah Chen
authorBio: Sarah Chen is a Physiotherapist and Rehab Specialist with expertise in sports injury and recovery protocols.

title: Why Every Athlete Should Practice Yoga
excerpt: How yoga improves flexibility, prevents injury, and enhances mental performance for athletes of all disciplines.
content: I used to think yoga was just stretching. As a former physiotherapist who worked with professional sports teams, I viewed it as a nice supplement but not essential. Then I actually studied it properly in India, and everything changed...
author: Elena Rodriguez
authorBio: Elena Rodriguez is a 500-hour certified yoga instructor who blends traditional yoga with modern mobility science.

title: The Mental Health Benefits of a Consistent Fitness Routine
excerpt: How regular exercise reduces anxiety, improves mood, and builds resilience — backed by neuroscience.
content: The connection between physical exercise and mental health is one of the most well-documented relationships in all of medicine. Regular physical activity has been shown to be as effective as medication for mild to moderate depression...
author: Rachel Nguyen
authorBio: Rachel Nguyen is a Mental Wellness Coach combining sports psychology with mindfulness at VitalEdge.

title: HIIT vs Steady-State Cardio: Which Burns More Fat?
excerpt: The science behind both approaches, who should use each, and how to combine them for maximum results.
content: The debate between HIIT and steady-state cardio has been raging for decades, and both sides have valid points. The truth? Neither is universally better. The right choice depends on your goals, fitness level, recovery capacity, and personal preference...
author: Marcus Williams
authorBio: Marcus Williams is a HIIT & Conditioning Coach specialising in fat loss and metabolic conditioning.

title: Supplements: What You Actually Need (and What's a Waste)
excerpt: A no-BS guide to supplements from a registered dietitian — what works, what doesn't, and what's worth your money.
content: The supplement industry is worth over $150 billion globally, and the vast majority of that money is spent on products that either don't work or aren't necessary. As a dietitian, I get asked about supplements daily. Here's my honest, evidence-based take...
author: David Okonkwo
authorBio: David Okonkwo is a Registered Dietitian and CSSD-certified sports dietitian at VitalEdge.

title: Returning to Fitness After Pregnancy: A Safe, Step-by-Step Guide
excerpt: What to expect, when to start, and how to rebuild your core and pelvic floor safely after childbirth.
content: The postpartum period is one of the most significant physical transitions a woman's body goes through...
author: Lisa Thompson
authorBio: Lisa Thompson is the Personal Training Lead at VitalEdge, specialising in women's fitness and pre/postnatal exercise.

title: 5-Minute Mobility Routines for Desk Workers
excerpt: Simple exercises you can do at your desk to combat the effects of prolonged sitting and prevent chronic pain.
content: Sitting for extended periods has become the new smoking. The average office worker spends over 8 hours a day seated, leading to tight hip flexors, rounded shoulders, and chronic lower back pain...
author: Sarah Chen
authorBio: Sarah Chen is a Physiotherapist and Rehab Specialist with expertise in workplace ergonomics and mobility.

title: How Much Protein Do You Actually Need? A Science-Backed Guide
excerpt: Cutting through the noise on protein requirements for muscle gain, fat loss, and general health.
content: Protein is the most debated macronutrient in fitness. Some say you need a shake every two hours, others claim the average person already eats too much. The truth, as always, lies somewhere in the middle and depends on your goals...
author: David Okonkwo
authorBio: David Okonkwo is a Registered Dietitian and CSSD-certified sports dietitian at VitalEdge.

title: Mindful Eating: Transform Your Relationship with Food
excerpt: How slowing down and paying attention to your meals can lead to better digestion, weight management, and enjoyment.
content: Most of us eat while scrolling, watching TV, or rushing between meetings. We've turned eating into an unconscious habit rather than an experience. Mindful eating is about reconnecting with your food...
author: Rachel Nguyen
authorBio: Rachel Nguyen is a Mental Wellness Coach combining sports psychology with mindfulness at VitalEdge.

title: Progressive Overload: The Key to Continuous Gains
excerpt: Understanding the most fundamental principle of strength training and how to apply it to your programme.
content: If you're doing the same workout with the same weights for the same reps month after month, you're not training — you're just exercising. Progressive overload is the non-negotiable principle that separates programmes that work from programmes that waste your time...
author: James Mitchell
authorBio: James Mitchell is a Senior Strength Coach at VitalEdge with 12 years of experience in strength development.

title: Hydration and Athletic Performance: What the Research Says
excerpt: Even mild dehydration can impair strength, endurance, and cognitive function. Here's how to stay optimally hydrated.
content: Water makes up about 60% of your body weight, and even a 2% drop in hydration can significantly impact physical and mental performance. Yet most people walk around chronically dehydrated without realising it...
author: David Okonkwo
authorBio: David Okonkwo is a Registered Dietitian specialising in sports nutrition and hydration strategies.

title: Breathwork Techniques for Stress and Recovery
excerpt: From box breathing to physiological sighs — evidence-based breathing methods to calm your nervous system.
content: Breathing is the one autonomic function you can consciously control. This gives you a direct line to your nervous system, allowing you to shift from a stressed, sympathetic state into a relaxed, parasympathetic one at will...
author: Elena Rodriguez
authorBio: Elena Rodriguez is a certified yoga instructor and breathwork facilitator at VitalEdge.

title: Foam Rolling: What Works, What Doesn't
excerpt: The evidence behind self-myofascial release and how to use a foam roller effectively for recovery.
content: Foam rolling has become a staple in warm-ups and cool-downs worldwide. But does it actually work? The research tells a nuanced story — it does improve range of motion and reduce perceived soreness, but not in the way most people think...
author: Sarah Chen
authorBio: Sarah Chen is a Physiotherapist with expertise in sports injury and recovery protocols.

title: Strength Training After 40: What Changes and What Stays the Same
excerpt: How to adapt your training as you age without sacrificing intensity or results.
content: Turning 40 doesn't mean it's time to swap the barbell for a light resistance band. In fact, strength training becomes even more critical as you age to combat sarcopenia, maintain bone density, and preserve metabolic health...
author: James Mitchell
authorBio: James Mitchell is a Senior Strength Coach at VitalEdge with 12 years of experience training clients across all age groups.
